attributes.action: Change the regulations for PET Travel from the UK to the EU
attributes.additional_details:
The current situation places financial and administrative burdens on ordinary people
and  it is very difficult to accept that the UK is being treated as a “third country” for pet travel
purposes, effectively grouped with countries that have very different animal health profiles. The UK is rabies-free and maintains extremely high animal health standards. The current policy does not reflect this reality and appears disproportionate.
attributes.background: The Government should negotiate a veterinary agreement with the European Union that would allow for mutual recognition of pet passports, or introduce a practical and reusable UK equivalent rather than having to issue a new Health Certificate for your pets every time you travel from the UK to the EU.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition, which calls for similar action: "Seek agreement with the EU to accept pet passports for UK-EU travel" https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/767906
